@@ -259,6 +259,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ori","title":"Task: Create lesson progress tracking system","description":"# Create Lesson Progress Tracking System ## Parent Feature Learning Hub Core Infrastructure (tor) ## Goal Implement a TanStack Query + localStorage based progress tracking system for the 9 lessons, following the pattern established by `lib/wizardSteps.ts`. ## Implementation ### File: `apps/web/lib/lessonProgress.ts` ```typescript import { useQuery, useMutation, useQueryClient } from "@tanstack/react-query"; import { useCallback } from "react"; import { safeGetJSON, safeSetJSON } from "./utils"; export interface Lesson { id: number; // 0-8 slug: string; // URL-friendly name title: string; // Display title file: string; // Source markdown file duration: string; // Estimated reading time description: string; // Brief summary } export const LESSONS: Lesson[] = [ { id: 0, slug: 'welcome', title: 'Welcome & Overview', file: '00_welcome.md', duration: '5 min', description: 'Understand what you have and what you will learn' }, { id: 1, slug: 'linux-basics', title: 'Linux Navigation', file: '01_linux_basics.md', duration: '8 min', description: 'Navigate the filesystem with confidence' }, { id: 2, slug: 'ssh-basics', title: 'SSH & Persistence', file: '02_ssh_basics.md', duration: '6 min', description: 'Stay connected and understand SSH' }, { id: 3, slug: 'tmux-basics', title: 'tmux Basics', file: '03_tmux_basics.md', duration: '7 min', description: 'Master persistent terminal sessions' }, { id: 4, slug: 'agent-commands', title: 'Agent Commands', file: '04_agents_login.md', duration: '10 min', description: 'Login and use your coding agents' }, { id: 5, slug: 'ntm-core', title: 'NTM Command Center', file: '05_ntm_core.md', duration: '8 min', description: 'Orchestrate agent sessions' }, { id: 6, slug: 'ntm-palette', title: 'NTM Prompt Palette', file: '06_ntm_command_palette.md', duration: '6 min', description: 'Use pre-built prompts effectively' }, { id: 7, slug: 'flywheel-loop', title: 'The Flywheel Loop', file: '07_flywheel_loop.md', duration: '10 min', description: 'Understand the complete workflow' }, { id: 8, slug: 'keeping-updated', title: 'Keeping Updated', file: '08_keeping_updated.md', duration: '4 min', description: 'Keep your tools current' }, ]; export const TOTAL_LESSONS = LESSONS.length; export const COMPLETED_LESSONS_KEY = "agent-flywheel-lessons-completed"; export const lessonKeys = { completedLessons: ["lessons", "completed"] as const, }; export function getLessonById(id: number): Lesson | undefined { return LESSONS.find(l => l.id === id); } export function getLessonBySlug(slug: string): Lesson | undefined { return LESSONS.find(l => l.slug === slug); } export function getCompletedLessons(): number[] { const parsed = safeGetJSON(COMPLETED_LESSONS_KEY); if (Array.isArray(parsed)) { return parsed.filter((n): n is number => typeof n === "number" && n >= 0 && n < TOTAL_LESSONS ); } return []; } export function setCompletedLessons(lessons: number[]): void { const valid = lessons.filter(n => n >= 0 && n < TOTAL_LESSONS); safeSetJSON(COMPLETED_LESSONS_KEY, valid); } export function useCompletedLessons(): [number[], (lessonId: number) => void] { const queryClient = useQueryClient(); const { data: lessons } = useQuery({ queryKey: lessonKeys.completedLessons, queryFn: getCompletedLessons, staleTime: Infinity, gcTime: Infinity, }); const mutation = useMutation({ mutationFn: async (lessonId: number) => { const current = getCompletedLessons(); if (current.includes(lessonId)) return current; const updated = [...current, lessonId].sort((a, b) => a - b); setCompletedLessons(updated); return updated; }, onSuccess: (updated) => { queryClient.setQueryData(lessonKeys.completedLessons, updated); }, }); const markComplete = useCallback((lessonId: number) => { mutation.mutate(lessonId); }, [mutation]); return [lessons ?? [], markComplete]; } // Helper for checking if a lesson is complete export function isLessonComplete(lessonId: number): boolean { return getCompletedLessons().includes(lessonId); } // Get next uncompleted lesson export function getNextLesson(): Lesson | undefined { const completed = getCompletedLessons(); return LESSONS.find(l => !completed.includes(l.id)); } ``` ## Testing Create `apps/web/lib/__tests__/lessonProgress.test.ts` with unit tests for: - `getLessonById` / `getLessonBySlug` - `getCompletedLessons` / `setCompletedLessons` - `getNextLesson` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All 9 lessons defined with correct metadata - [ ] Hook works identically to wizard progress hook - [ ] Progress persists across page reloads - [ ] TypeScript types are properly exported ## File Changes - Create: `apps/web/lib/lessonProgress.ts` - Create: `apps/web/lib/__tests__/lessonProgress.test.ts` (optional)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:19:02.691384-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:25:36.032502-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:25:36.032502-05:00","close_reason":"Implementation complete - lessonProgress.ts already exists with all 9 lessons, TanStack Query hooks, localStorage persistence, and TypeScript types. Verified type-check passes.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ori","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-tor","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:06.880428-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-pom","title":"Implement ACFS self-update","description":"## What Before updating other components, acfs-update should update itself: 1. git pull in ~/.acfs (or wherever ACFS is installed) 2. Re-source the update script if it changed 3. Continue with other updates using latest logic ## Why This Matters - Ensures we have the latest update logic before updating tools - Fixes any bugs in the update process itself - Keeps checksums.yaml current (though not used for updates) ## Technical Details - ACFS installed at ~/.acfs or detected via ACFS_HOME env var - git pull --ff-only to avoid merge conflicts - If update script changed, need to re-exec with same args ## Considerations - What if git pull fails? (network, conflicts) - Should we require this, or make it optional? - Re-exec is tricky - need to preserve all args and state ## Decision Make self-update automatic but non-fatal. If it fails, warn and continue with current version. ## Success Criteria - [ ] ACFS self-updates on each run - [ ] Handles git pull failures gracefully - [ ] Re-exec works if script changed (or skip re-exec v1)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:26:45.271437-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:31:43.349328-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T13:31:43.349328-05:00","close_reason":"Out of scope for v1. ACFS is not git-cloned to ~/.acfs, so self-update requires re-running the installer. Users can do this manually. Consider for v2 after changing install to use git clone.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-pom","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:27:25.670496-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-q10","title":"Harden installer: true dry-run, onboard source, HTTPS PGDG","description":"Fix install.sh --dry-run so it makes no system changes and works without sudo; install onboard command from packages/onboard/onboard.sh (single source of truth); switch PGDG apt repo URL to HTTPS; fix checksum computation in scripts/lib/security.sh.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"FuchsiaCreek","created_at":"2025-12-20T13:18:40.054877-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T13:22:16.261425-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T13:22:16.261425-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed install.sh dry-run to make no system changes + allow running without sudo; install onboard from packages/onboard; switch PGDG repo to HTTPS; fix security.sh checksum logic."} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-q6w2","title":"Installer: initialize selection arrays early","description":"install.sh uses selection arrays (ONLY_MODULES/ONLY_PHASES/SKIP_MODULES/NO_DEPS) under set -u. While install_helpers.sh usually initializes them when sourced, initializing them directly in install.sh prevents any unbound-variable edge cases and makes the script self-contained.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:45.835164-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T22:59:19.848047-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T22:59:19.848047-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: added explicit jq failure handling in state.sh write/update helpers and initialized selection arrays early in install.sh; shellcheck + bash -n passed."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-q7r3","title":"[EPIC] First SSH Connection Experience","description":"# Epic: First SSH Connection Experience ## Problem Statement The first SSH connection is where the most beginners fail and abandon the process. Three critical issues: 1. **"Type yes" terror**: The host authenticity warning looks like a SECURITY ALERT. Users are afraid to type "yes" because it looks like they're accepting malware. 2. **Password confusion**: Users confuse their VPS provider account password (Hetzner login) with the VPS root password (set during VPS creation). These are DIFFERENT passwords. 3. **Post-connection disorientation**: User successfully connects, sees "root@vps:~#", has no idea what this means or that they're now "inside" the VPS. ## Background & Reasoning SSH is a completely foreign concept. "Secure Shell" means nothing to beginners. The mental model of "remote controlling another computer through text" is not intuitive. The host authenticity prompt is particularly problematic because it uses security language ("ECDSA key fingerprint", "Are you sure you want to continue?") that sounds dangerous. Security-conscious users will hesitate; naive users will learn bad habits. The password confusion is a classic support issue. Users try their Hetzner account password 3 times, get locked out, and panic. ## User Story As a beginner making my first SSH connection, I want clear guidance through each prompt and potential error, So that I can successfully connect without fear or confusion. ## Success Criteria 1. User understands the "type yes" prompt is normal and safe 2. User knows which password to use (VPS root, not provider account) 3. User understands they're now "inside" the VPS after connecting 4. User can verify they're on the VPS (e.g., run hostname) 5. User knows how to disconnect (exit) ## Scope - Enhance ssh-connect wizard page with more detailed guidance - Add prominent password clarification - Add post-connection verification step - Improve troubleshooting for common errors - Add mental model explanation for "being inside" the VPS ## Dependencies - Epic A: Terminal Onboarding Foundation (user must understand terminal) - Epic B: SSH Key Generation (user must have generated key) ## UI/UX Requirements - Maintain current polish for desktop and mobile - Password clarification should be PROMINENT, not buried in SimplerGuide - Use AlertCard for critical warnings (which password to use) - Consider showing the scary prompt so users recognize it ## Technical Approach 1. Add AlertCard above the SSH command explaining password distinction 2. Add OutputPreview showing the "type yes" prompt with explanation 3. Add post-connection verification: "Try running: hostname" 4. Enhance troubleshooting with password-specific guidance 5. Add "You're now inside the VPS" confirmation section ## Estimated Effort Medium (1 wizard page major enhancement) ## Priority Justification P0 (Critical) - This is the highest drop-off point in the wizard. Users who fail here never complete setup.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:34:56.174103-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:48:02.756085-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:48:02.756085-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented: Added prominent password distinction warning (VPS root vs provider account), 'type yes' prompt preview with explanation, password prompt with invisible typing note. All success criteria met.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-q7r3","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ux1f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:37:43.878369-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-q7r3","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-1wk6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:37:49.119933-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-qi82","title":"FEATURE: Static Dashboard Generation (Offline Reference)","description":"# FEATURE: Static Dashboard Generation (Offline Reference) ## Purpose Generate a self-contained HTML dashboard that works completely offline, providing users with a persistent reference page they can access anytime—even without SSH access to their VPS. ## Background & Rationale ### Why Not a Web Server? We explicitly decided AGAINST running a persistent web server because: 1. **Security surface**: Password-only auth is weak 2. **Complexity**: Port management, firewall rules, TLS certificates 3. **Scope creep**: ACFS sets up dev environments, not production web services 4. **Beginner risk**: Users might not understand the exposure ### The Static Alternative Instead, we generate a static HTML file that: - Lives at `~/.acfs/dashboard/index.html` - Contains all personalized info (IP, hostname, tools, aliases) - Works offline when saved locally - Can be served on-demand when needed - Requires no persistent process ## User Workflows ### Workflow 1: Quick Local Reference ```bash # Generate/update the dashboard acfs dashboard generate # Open directly in terminal browser (if available) open ~/.acfs/dashboard/index.html # macOS xdg-open ~/.acfs/dashboard/index.html # Linux ``` ### Workflow 2: Access from Another Device ```bash # SSH into VPS ssh ubuntu@203.0.113.42 # Start temporary server acfs dashboard serve # → Serving at http://0.0.0.0:8080 # → Press Ctrl+C to stop # From laptop browser, visit http://203.0.113.42:8080 ``` ### Workflow 3: Save Offline Copy ```bash # On VPS, generate latest acfs dashboard generate # From laptop, copy to local scp ubuntu@203.0.113.42:~/.acfs/dashboard/index.html ./my-vps-reference.html # Open locally anytime open ./my-vps-reference.html ``` ## Implementation ### Generate Command ```bash acfs dashboard generate [--force] ``` Internally runs: ```bash acfs info --html > ~/.acfs/dashboard/index.html ``` ### Serve Command ```bash acfs dashboard serve [--port 8080] ``` Internally runs: ```bash cd ~/.acfs/dashboard && python3 -m http.server 8080 ``` With messaging: ``` ╭─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮ │ 📊 ACFS Dashboard Server │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ Serving at: http://0.0.0.0:8080 │ │ Local URL: http://localhost:8080 │ │ Press Ctrl+C to stop │ │ │ │ ⚠️ This is a temporary server for quick access. │ │ It will stop when you press Ctrl+C or close this terminal. │ ╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯ ``` ### HTML Template Design The generated HTML should: 1. Be self-contained (inline CSS, no external dependencies) 2. Work offline (no CDN links) 3. Be responsive (mobile-friendly) 4. Use dark theme (match terminal aesthetic) 5. Include all info from `acfs info` output 6. Have copyable command snippets 7. Include timestamp of generation ### Content Sections 1. **Header**: Hostname, IP, generation timestamp 2. **System Info**: OS version, uptime, resources 3. **Installed Tools**: Categorized list with status 4. **Quick Commands**: Copyable aliases with descriptions 5. **Onboard Progress**: Current lesson, completion percentage 6. **Troubleshooting Links**: Quick access to doctor, logs 7. **Footer**: Regeneration instructions, ACFS version ## File Structure ``` ~/.acfs/dashboard/ ├── index.html # Main dashboard (generated) └── .last_generated # Timestamp for staleness check ``` ## Auto-Regeneration (Optional Enhancement) Could add hook to regenerate on: - `acfs update` completion - Onboard lesson completion - Weekly cron (if user opts in) For now, manual regeneration is fine. ## Dependencies - `acfs info --html` (provides the content) - Python 3 (for http.server, already installed) ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] `acfs dashboard generate` creates valid HTML - [ ] `acfs dashboard serve` starts temporary server - [ ] Generated HTML works when opened as file:// URL - [ ] Generated HTML works when served via HTTP - [ ] HTML is self-contained (no external resources) - [ ] Content matches `acfs info` output - [ ] Dark theme, readable on mobile - [ ] Copy buttons work for commands - [ ] Timestamp shows when generated ## Security Considerations ### Serve Command Warnings - Show clear warning that server is temporary - Bind to 0.0.0.0 by default (accessible externally) - No authentication (acceptable for temporary access) - Emphasize Ctrl+C to stop ### Sensitive Information - IP address is already exposed (user knows it) - No secrets/passwords in dashboard - Tool versions are not sensitive - Onboard progress is not sensitive ## Design Decisions ### Why Python's http.server? - Already installed (Python 3 is on every Ubuntu) - No additional dependencies - Simple, well-understood - Good enough for occasional use ### Why Not a Fancier Server? - Not serving production traffic - No need for HTTPS (temporary access) - No need for auth (user controls when it runs) - Simplicity > features for this use case ### Why Generate to File vs Serve Dynamically? - File can be copied offline - No server process needed for local viewing - Can be regenerated anytime - Serves as snapshot of current state","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:55:11.602274-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T15:22:25.874136-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T15:22:25.874136-05:00","close_reason":"Complete: dashboard.sh with generate command, uses info.sh --html for HTML generation","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-qi82","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-bags","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:55:22.756946-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ql6q","title":"Explain RAM and specs in practical user terms","description":"# Task: Explain RAM and Specs in User Terms ## Parent Epic [EPIC] VPS Conceptual Framework ## Problem "16GB RAM" and "Ubuntu 24.04" are meaningless to beginners. They don't know: - What RAM means in practical terms - Why more is better for AI agents - What Ubuntu version numbers mean - What to do if their provider doesn't have 25.10 ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/create-vps/page.tsx Enhance the checklist and SimplerGuide: \`\`\`tsx

RAM (Memory)

RAM is your VPS's "working memory" — how many things it can think about at once. More RAM = more AI agents running simultaneously.

  • 8GB: Minimum. One AI agent at a time.
  • 16GB: Comfortable. 2-3 agents working together.
  • 32GB: Ideal. Run agent swarms without worry.

Ubuntu Version

Ubuntu is a type of Linux (the operating system). The numbers are version identifiers (like iPhone 15 vs iPhone 14). Pick the highest number available. Our installer will upgrade to 25.10 if needed.

\`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] RAM explained with practical examples (agents = tasks) - [ ] Clear tier recommendations (8/16/32GB) - [ ] Ubuntu versions demystified - [ ] Reassurance about installer handling upgrades ## Why This Matters Users who don't understand specs either over-buy (wasting money) or under-buy (causing performance issues later).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:54:26.539739-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:02:06.572034-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:02:06.572034-05:00","close_reason":"Already covered: rent-vps/page.tsx has extensive 'Understanding the specs' and 'Why 64GB RAM?' sections"} @@ -269,6 +270,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-qzwz","title":"Fix BV robot triage command flags in tools page","description":"Fix the BV robot triage command example to use the correct double-dash flags (e.g., 'bv --robot-triage --recipe high-impact') in apps/web/app/learn/tools/[tool]/page.tsx.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:50:36.307735-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:56:03.461043-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:56:03.461043-05:00","close_reason":"Not a bug: bv uses single-dash flags (-robot-triage, -recipe)"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r2np","title":"[EPIC] Post-Install Transition Experience","description":"# Epic: Post-Install Transition Experience ## Problem Statement After installation, users must reconnect as ubuntu (instead of root). This transition has several pitfalls: 1. **New SSH command format**: The reconnect command uses `-i ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519` which is NEW. Users don't understand they're now using key auth instead of password. 2. **Key auth failure**: If key auth doesn't work, they're stuck. No clear fallback path. 3. **Powerlevel10k wizard surprise**: On first zsh login, p10k may launch a configuration wizard. This is unexpected and confusing. 4. **Prompt change disorientation**: The prompt looks completely different now (colorful p10k vs plain bash). Users might think something is wrong. ## Background & Reasoning The root → ubuntu transition is a security best practice, but it creates a jarring experience. Users went from password auth as root to key auth as ubuntu. The shell also changes from bash to zsh with p10k theming. Each of these changes is an improvement, but together they make the VPS feel like a completely different machine. Users need preparation for these changes. ## User Story As a user reconnecting after installation, I want to understand the changes to my VPS, So that I'm not confused by the new authentication method, shell, and prompt. ## Success Criteria 1. User understands the new SSH command uses their key 2. User knows they won't need a password anymore 3. User knows to press 'q' if p10k wizard appears 4. User understands the colorful prompt is normal and good 5. User has a fallback if key auth fails ## Scope - Enhance reconnect-ubuntu page with key vs password explanation - Add p10k wizard handling instructions - Add "Your prompt looks different now" explanation - Add key auth troubleshooting - Add fallback instructions if key doesn't work ## Dependencies - Epic F: Installation (user must have completed installation) ## UI/UX Requirements - Maintain current polish for desktop and mobile - Key auth explanation should be clear but not overwhelming - P10k handling should be a GuideTip - Prompt change should include before/after visual ## Technical Approach 1. Add explanation of key auth vs password above SSH command 2. Add GuideTip about p10k wizard (press q to skip) 3. Add OutputPreview showing new prompt style 4. Add troubleshooting section for key auth failures 5. Consider adding visual comparison: old prompt vs new ## Estimated Effort Small-Medium (1 wizard page enhancement) ## Priority Justification P1 (High) - Reconnection failure after successful install is very frustrating.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:36:12.00915-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:55:04.567741-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:55:04.567741-05:00","close_reason":"All 5 success criteria verified: (1) key auth explanation in reconnect-ubuntu:131-141, (2) 'No password needed' AlertCard:143-146, (3) p10k wizard handling:170-189, (4) colorful prompt explained:165,203,235, (5) key auth fallback/troubleshooting in verify-key-connection:120-137","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r2np","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vydu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:38:04.85656-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r3gd","title":"Align agent aliases across zshrc and docs","description":"Found mismatch: zsh aliases for cod/gmi didn't match AGENTS/README/onboarding. Update acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc, onboarding lesson 04, and agent command examples to use codex --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox and gemini --yolo; align README snippet.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:15:58.950788-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:30:11.495408-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T19:30:11.495408-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 45ea80d - namespaced SCRIPT_DIR and aligned agent aliases"} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r50f","title":"State library: fail clearly on jq update errors","description":"scripts/lib/state.sh has several jq command substitutions that don’t check exit status (e.g., state_step_update/state_phase_complete/state_phase_fail/state_save/state_migrate_v1_to_v2/state_upgrade_set_error). In non-strict callers this can silently propagate bad state updates. Add explicit error handling so failures return nonzero without writing invalid/empty state.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:54:49.303573-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T22:59:19.847092-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T22:59:19.847092-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: added explicit jq failure handling in state.sh write/update helpers and initialized selection arrays early in install.sh; shellcheck + bash -n passed."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r56u","title":"TASK: Integrate acfs info into CLI and zshrc","description":"# TASK: Integrate acfs info into CLI and zshrc ## Context Wire up the info command into the existing acfs CLI function. ## Implementation ### Update acfs() function in acfs.zshrc ```bash acfs() { case "$1" in info) shift source "${ACFS_HOME:-$HOME/.acfs}/scripts/lib/info.sh" acfs_info "$@" ;; # ... existing cases esac } ``` ### Update acfs help output Add info to the help text: ``` acfs info Show environment quick reference acfs info --json Output as JSON acfs info --html Output as HTML ``` ### Create acfs_info dispatcher ```bash acfs_info() { case "$1" in --json) info_render_json ;; --html) info_render_html ;; --minimal) info_render_minimal ;; --help) info_show_help ;; *) info_render_terminal ;; esac } ``` ## Testing ```bash acfs info # Terminal output acfs info --json # JSON output acfs info --json | jq # Validate JSON acfs info --html > /tmp/test.html # HTML output acfs help # Shows info in list ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] acfs info works - [ ] acfs info --json works - [ ] acfs info --html works - [ ] acfs help shows info command - [ ] Help text is accurate","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:57:30.415862-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T15:07:45.631863-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T15:07:45.631863-05:00","close_reason":"Complete implementation: info.sh with terminal, JSON, HTML, minimal output formats; integrated into acfs CLI","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r56u","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-lwu7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:57:52.815793-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r6i3","title":"Fix acfs update: MCP Agent Mail tmux command argument splitting","description":"scripts/lib/update.sh uses tmux new-session with a single quoted string (" --dir ..."), so tmux tries to exec a binary with spaces. Pass command + args as separate argv entries (e.g., bash --dir /Users/jemanuel/mcp_agent_mail --yes).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:27:50.18235-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T22:31:32.626103-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T22:31:32.626103-05:00","close_reason":"Completed"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r7xo","title":"Default-collapse non-essential service tiers on accounts page","description":"# Task: Improve Accounts Page Information Hierarchy ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Authentication Flow for Headless VPS ## Problem The accounts page shows 8+ services at once, which is overwhelming. Even with tiers, seeing everything expanded makes users feel like they need ALL the services before proceeding. ## Current State - Essential tier: expanded (correct) - Recommended tier: expanded (should be collapsed) - Optional tier: expanded (should be collapsed) ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/accounts/page.tsx Change TIER_META defaults: \`\`\`tsx const TIER_META: Record = { essential: { // ... defaultOpen: true, // Keep open - these are required }, recommended: { // ... defaultOpen: false, // CHANGE: Collapse by default }, optional: { // ... defaultOpen: false, // Keep collapsed }, }; \`\`\` Also add clearer messaging: \`\`\`tsx Focus on Essential first. You only need GitHub and Claude Code to get started.
